name: About Bagels-- General Directions 2 A
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup liquid for yeast and sugar (110-115°F)
Remaining liquid, very cool (out of the refrigerator)
Active dry yeast or fast-rising yeast
Water or ice cubes (for cooling liquid)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 1/4 cup liquid to 110-115°F in microwave and add to yeast and sugar in a small cup. Mix gently and let sit for 5 minutes. Pour remaining liquid, cooled right out of the refrigerator, or add an ice cube into another cup.
2. Pulse flour and salt with metal cutting blade in food processor bowl until mixed. Add oil or butter and pulse until incorporated.
3. Pour yeast mixture through feeding tube into flour and pulse to form a ball. Pulse a few more times to knead. When dough comes away from sides, it is ready.
4. Remove dough from processor bowl and hand-knead to remove gases. Let rest for about 5 minutes. Adjust consistency with water or flour if needed for elasticity.
5. First rise: Place dough in microwave-safe bowl, wrap tightly, and heat on LOW (30%) or DEFROST setting for 1 minute. Rest for 10 minutes, then repeat until doubled. Alternatively, place dough in processor bowl, cover with damp tea towel or plastic wrap, and heat on LOW (30%) for 3 minutes, rest for 6 minutes, repeating as necessary.
6. Second rise: Cover dough lightly oiled and sprayed with plastic wrap in a large bowl and let rise until doubled, about 1 to 2 hours.
Proceed to Step 2: Shape Bagels.
